prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response: Arrr! Listen close, ye scallywag, and I’ll tell ye why the sky be wearin’ that sapphire hue above our heads! ‘Tis the work o’ the sun’s rays—them golden beams—bouncin’ off the very air itself! But not all rays be equal, mind ye. The blue ones, bein’ the spryest and most nimble o’ the lot, scatter far and wide through the heavens like cannon shot through fog. The reds and yellows? They be stout and lazy, plodding straight on like a drunken bosun. So when ye squint up at midday, ‘tis the blue light—scattered every which way by the very breath o’ the sky—that fills yer peepers! Aye, ‘tis nature’s own trick, not sorcery nor the tears o’ Neptune—but light dancin’ with the wind itself! Now quit yer gawkin’ and swab the deck, or I’ll make ye walk the plank into that very blue yonder!
